title: Copernicus Global Human Settlement Layer (GHSL) — Metadata Record
abstract: Metadata record describing the Copernicus Global Human Settlement Layer (GHSL), a global gridded dataset of built-up surface and population produced by the European Commission Joint Research Centre (JRC) from Sentinel-2/Landsat imagery and census data. Only the dataset metadata record has been downloaded locally; the underlying GHS-BUILT-S and GHS-POP raster grids themselves (2.0 GB and 4.9 GB global downloads respectively) have not yet been acquired.

resources.name: GHSL dataset metadata (CSV, HDX-style field/label/value export)

variables.name: resource_1_description
variables.label: GHS-BUILT-S Description
variables.description: Describes the GHS-BUILT-S built-up surface product: Epoch 2025, 100m resolution, Mollweide projection, residential/non-residential classification.

variables.name: resource_2_description
variables.label: GHS-POP Description
variables.description: Describes the GHS-POP population grid product: Epoch 2025, 100m resolution, Mollweide projection, multitemporal 1975-2030.

variables.name: resource_download_url
variables.label: Download URL
variables.description: Direct download link to the JRC JEODPP FTP archive for each GHSL product (not yet fetched into local storage).

access.how_to_request: Freely downloadable from the JRC GHSL data portal (https://ghsl.jrc.ec.europa.eu/) and JEODPP FTP archive; no request required. Local KAUST copy currently holds only the dataset metadata, not the raster grids.

provenance.citation: Schiavina M., Freire S., Carioli A., MacManus K. (2023): GHS-POP R2023A - GHS population grid multitemporal (1975-2030). European Commission, Joint Research Centre (JRC).

known_issues: Only the dataset metadata CSV is present locally; the actual GHS-BUILT-S and GHS-POP GeoTIFF grids (2.0 GB and 4.9 GB) have not been downloaded yet.
GHSL is a global product with no Red-Sea-coastal-specific subsetting or clipping performed locally.

A separate GHS-BUILT-S citation/DOI also applies (Pesaresi & Politis 2023, doi:10.2905/9F06F36F-4B11-47EC-ABB0-4F8B7B1D72EA) but is not included here since this record's citation privileges the population product most relevant to the socio-economic domain.
